verdict_rationale: Zero CRITICAL findings and zero un-mitigated MAJOR findings: every prior MAJOR (CQ-01..CQ-05, CQ-07, CQ-14) has a tracked behavioural test that asserts the closure, and the remaining structural smells are now MINOR/INFO.
remediation_diff_summary: All twelve prior MAJOR/WARNING findings are closed. The 612-line monolith is now a 7-file package whose largest unit (parse.py, 262 lines) and longest function (parse_mcp_server_json, 26 lines) are well under thresholds. CQ-01 (extras back-door), CQ-02 (silent JSON→TOML fallback), CQ-03 (catch-all in extract_pair_id), CQ-05 (long parse function), CQ-07 (transport-fork divergence), CQ-09 (long _render_http_headers), CQ-14 (round-trip assertion gap), and CQ-16 (coverage gap on sse/streamable-http/TOML/bearer/env_http_headers) each have a dedicated regression test. No regressions detected; residual W3/W8 primitive-obsession on the canonical-dict schema persists but is bounded to the package and is now INFO-grade.
detailed_analysis: The remediation has materially improved this slice. The monolithic mcp_server_io.py (612 lines, multifaceted responsibility) is now a cohesive package whose modules each own one concern: dialect (data class), _slot_codec (format I/O), _helpers (pure value transforms), headers (HTTP-header policy), parse (slot → canonical), render (canonical → slot), __init__ (re-export). Every prior MAJOR finding has a corresponding behavioural test in tests/test_mcp_server_io.py. CQ-01 (extras back-door): closed via test_mcp_secret_policy_refuse_flags_secret_extra_exactly and test_mcp_secret_policy_redact_rewrites_secret_extra_before_persisting. CQ-02 (silent JSON→TOML fallback): closed; _slot_codec.loads_slot now dispatches on an explicit slot_format kwarg. CQ-03 (extract_pair_id catch-all): closed; JSONDecodeError and ValueError propagate. CQ-04 (Dialect god-class): partially mitigated structurally. CQ-05 (long parse function): closed (26 lines, six helpers). CQ-06 (dead artifact_root parameter): persists as INFO. CQ-07 (divergent transport switches): closed via _copy_transport_fields/_render_transport_fields. CQ-08/CQ-10/CQ-12 (primitive obsession): persist as INFO. CQ-09 (long _render_http_headers): closed (23 lines). CQ-11 (canonical_transport rebuild): persists as INFO. CQ-13 (renderer discards redactions): persists as INFO. CQ-14 (round-trip assertion gap): closed. CQ-15 (assertion-roulette): partially closed via list comprehension. CQ-16 (coverage gap): closed via new tests for sse, streamable-http, TOML, bearer, env_http_headers, prior_canonical reuse, unknown-transport rejection. No regressions. The package is structurally sound and ready to absorb v0.5 tool adapters.

findings.description: parse_mcp_server_json still accepts an artifact_root parameter that is immediately discarded with `del artifact_root`. Persists from prior CQ-06.
findings.recommendation: Either drop artifact_root from the public signature now that no caller relies on it, or wire it through to apply_mcp_secret_policy as the artifact context when artifact_path is relative.

findings.description: render_mcp_server_json discards the redactions tuple from apply_mcp_secret_policy with `obj, _ = ...`. If the renderer ever produces a fresh redaction, that signal is lost.
findings.recommendation: Either assert `len(redactions) == 0` at render time or surface the redactions on the return so the caller can log them.

findings.category: W8 — Primitive obsession on tool_only schema
findings.description: The tool_only dict still uses nine stringly-typed magic keys that writer and readers must agree on by convention.
findings.recommendation: Introduce a TypedDict or frozen dataclass `ToolOnlySpellings` so mypy --strict catches key drift.

findings.description: McpServerDialect.canonical_transport rebuilds the aliases dict from self.transport_aliases on every call. The dialect is frozen, so this is pure waste.
findings.recommendation: Compute the aliases lookup in __post_init__ or via functools.cached_property so the dict is built once per dialect instance.

findings.category: W3 — Inappropriate intimacy with canonical dict
findings.description: clear_mcp_fields and known_slot_fields still encode the canonical schema as two parallel literal sets — adding a new canonical field requires updating both.
findings.recommendation: Promote the canonical schema to a CanonicalMcpServer dataclass (or TypedDict) and derive both clear_mcp_fields and known_slot_fields from it via dataclasses.fields() or __annotations__.
